title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

description: Experience the rustic charm of authentic Focaccia Genovese, a beloved Italian flatbread. Infused with aromatic rosemary and rich olive oil, this tender, airy bread boasts a crispy crust and a delightfully chewy interior. It's perfect for dipping, sandwiches, or as a flavorful accompaniment to any meal.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active. This is crucial for a light and airy focaccia.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour, 1 teaspoon of fine sea salt, and 1/4 cup of the extra virgin olive oil to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or a stand mixer with a dough hook until a shaggy dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface or with the mixer for 5-7 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. This slow rise develops flavor.

instructions.title: Shape and Dimple
instructions.text: Pour the remaining 1/4 cup of olive oil into a 9x13 inch baking pan, ensuring the bottom is well coated. Gently transfer the risen dough to the pan, turning it once to coat with oil. Using your fingertips, gently stretch the dough to fill the pan, creating characteristic dimples across the surface. Sprinkle with chopped fresh rosemary.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Topping
instructions.text: Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a kitchen towel and let the dough rise again for 30-45 minutes in a warm place, or until visibly puffy. Just before baking, sprinkle the dough with the remaining 1 teaspoon of fine sea salt or flaky sea salt.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 425F (220C). Bake the focaccia for 25-35 minutes, or until it is golden brown on top and cooked through. The bottom should also be nicely browned and crisp. Once baked, remove from the oven and immediately drizzle with a little extra virgin olive oil for shine and flavor.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Allow the focaccia to cool in the pan for 5-10 minutes before transferring it to a wire rack to cool completely. Slice into squares or rectangles and serve warm or at room temperature. This focaccia is best enjoyed fresh on the day it's baked.

tips: For an extra tender crumb, use a high-quality Italian Tipo 00 flour, though all-purpose flour works wonderfully too.
Don't be shy with the olive oil in the pan; it's key to the focaccia's crispy bottom and rich flavor profile.
Leftover focaccia can be stored at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days, or frozen for longer storage. Reheat gently in the oven.
article:
The aromatic embrace of freshly baked bread is a universal pleasure, but few breads capture the heart and soul quite like Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil. Originating from the sun-drenched Ligurian coast of Italy, this iconic flatbread is more than just a simple dough; it's a testament to the region's culinary philosophy, where simple, high-quality ingredients combine to create something truly extraordinary. Imagine a golden crust, crisp yet yielding, giving way to an impossibly soft, airy interior, all infused with the pungent perfume of rosemary and the rich, fruity notes of extra virgin olive oil. This isn't just bread; it's an experience, a journey to the heart of Italian baking that you can recreate in your own kitchen.

## What is Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil?

At its core, Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a style of focaccia hailing specifically from Genoa, the capital of Liguria. What sets it apart from other focaccia variations is its distinct character: a remarkably high hydration dough, a generous use of quality extra virgin olive oil both within the dough and brushed on top, and the signature "dimples" that create pockets of golden crispness. Unlike some denser breads, Genovese focaccia is celebrated for its incredibly light, open crumb and a delicate chewiness. The addition of fresh rosemary isnary to its classic flavor profile, imparting an earthy, piney aroma that perfectly complements the olive oil. Coarse sea salt sprinkled generously on top provides a delightful textural contrast and a burst of savory flavor, completing this humble yet sophisticated masterpiece. It's a staple in Ligurian bakeries, often enjoyed for breakfast, a snack, or alongside meals.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil doesn't require an extensive list of ingredients, but the quality of each element truly shines through. Here’s what you’ll need to embark on this delicious baking adventure:

*   **All-Purpose Flour or "00" Flour:** While all-purpose works well, "00" flour (a finely milled Italian flour) will give you an even softer, more tender crumb.
*   **Warm Water:** Essential for activating the yeast. Ensure it's between 105-115°F (40-46°C).
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** The leavening agent that gives the focaccia its airy texture.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** Incorporated into the dough for flavor.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil (EVOO):** This is non-negotiable. Use a high-quality, flavorful EVOO. It's used in the dough, for oiling the pan, brushing the top, and finishing.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** Stripped from the stems and roughly chopped. Dried rosemary can be a substitute, but fresh is highly recommended for its potent aroma.
*   **Coarse Sea Salt (e.g., Flaky Sea Salt or Maldon):** For sprinkling on top before baking, providing a delightful crunch and burst of salinity.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a rewarding process that emphasizes patience and simple techniques.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the fine sea salt and about half of the extra virgin olive oil to the yeast mixture. Gradually add the flour, mixing with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy, sticky dough forms. This is a high-hydration dough, so don't be alarmed by its stickiness – avoid adding too much extra flour.
3.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap or a damp cloth. Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
4.  **Prepare the Baking Pan:** Drizzle a generous amount of extra virgin olive oil (about 1/4 cup) into a 9x13 inch baking pan (or a similar size). Ensure the entire bottom and sides are well coated.
5.  **Transfer and Stretch:** Gently pour the risen dough into the prepared pan. Using your oiled fingertips, gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. If the dough resists, let it rest for 10 minutes and try again.
6.  **The Signature Dimples:** Once the dough is mostly stretched, drizzle another generous amount of extra virgin olive oil over the surface. Use your fingers to create deep dimples all over the dough, pressing all the way down to the bottom of the pan. These pockets will hold the olive oil and create those wonderful crispy bits.
7.  **Second Rise (Proofing):** Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel. Let the dough proof for another 30-45 minutes in a warm place, or until it looks puffy and airy.
8.  **Preheat Oven & Top:** While the dough is proofing, preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Once proofed, sprinkle the fresh rosemary evenly over the surface of the dough, followed by the coarse sea salt.
9.  **Bake:** Bake for 20-30 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown on top and the edges are crisp.
10. **Finish with Olive Oil:** As soon as you remove the Focaccia Genovese Rosemary from the oven, drizzle a little more high-quality extra virgin olive oil over the hot surface. This enhances the flavor and keeps the crust tender.
11. **Cool and Serve:** Let the focaccia cool in the pan for a few minutes before transferring it to a wire rack. Slice into squares or rectangles and serve warm.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, airy Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is a blend of technique and patience. Here are some pro tips to elevate your baking:

*   **Embrace the Stickiness:** Focaccia dough is meant to be very wet and sticky. Resist the urge to add excessive flour, which will result in a dry, dense focaccia. Oiling your hands and working surface is key to managing it.
*   **Quality Olive Oil is Paramount:** Since olive oil is such a dominant flavor, investing in a good quality extra virgin olive oil will make a noticeable difference in the final taste and aroma of your focaccia. Don't skimp!
*   **Patience with Proofing:** Rushing the rise times will lead to a less airy focaccia. Ensure your dough has genuinely doubled in size for the first rise and is visibly puffy for the second. A warm, draft-free spot is ideal.
*   **The Power of Dimples:** Don't be shy when creating the dimples. Press your fingers firmly down to the bottom of the pan. These indentations are crucial for trapping the olive oil and creating the characteristic texture.
*   **Hot Oven, Quick Bake:** Focaccia bakes best in a hot oven. This helps create that desirable crispy crust while keeping the interior soft and moist.
*   **Generous Seasoning:** Don't be afraid to use plenty of fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt. They are essential components of the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary flavor profile.
*   **Listen to Your Dough:** While times are a guide, pay attention to how your dough feels and looks. It should be light, bubbly, and full of air.

## Serving Suggestions

The beauty of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is its versatility. It can be the star of the show or a perfect supporting act.

*   **As an Appetizer:** Serve warm with a side of good quality balsamic vinegar for dipping, or with a selection of olives and cured meats.
*   **With Meals:** It's an excellent accompaniment to soups, stews, salads, or pasta dishes, perfect for soaking up delicious sauces.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally to create the most incredible sandwich bread. Fill it with prosciutto, fresh mozzarella, roasted vegetables, or a simple tomato and basil salad.
*   **Breakfast or Snack:** In Genoa, it's common to enjoy a slice of focaccia with a cup of coffee or as a quick, satisfying snack throughout the day.
*   **Cheese Board:** A fantastic addition to any cheese board, especially with soft cheeses like burrata or creamy goat cheese.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is perfect as is, there's always room for creative twists:

*   **Other Herbs:** Experiment with other fresh herbs like thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs instead of or in addition to rosemary.
*   **Toppings Galore:** Before the final bake, add other Ligurian-inspired toppings such as cherry tomatoes (halved), thinly sliced red onion, black olives, or even a sprinkle of grated Parmesan cheese.
*   **Sweet Focaccia:** While less common for the Genovese style, some focaccias are made sweet with sugar, grapes, or figs.
*   **Whole Wheat Flour:** For a nuttier flavor and added fiber, substitute up to a quarter of the all-purpose flour with whole wheat flour.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While challenging, specific gluten-free flour blends designed for yeast breads can be used, though the texture will be different.
*   **Vegan-Friendly:** This recipe is naturally vegan, making it a wonderful option for those avoiding animal products. Just ensure your olive oil is plant-based, which it always is!

Embracing the tradition of Italian baking, especially when it comes to a classic like Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil, is a truly rewarding endeavor. From the first pour of olive oil to the final golden slice, each step is a celebration of simple ingredients transformed into something extraordinary. Whether you're a seasoned baker or just starting your journey, this recipe offers a chance to create a piece of culinary art that will delight your senses and transport you straight to the sun-drenched streets of Genoa. So roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to bake a masterpiece that tastes of tradition, passion, and pure deliciousness.
